questions.question: The groundbreaking 'Transformer' neural network architecture, which revolutionized natural language processing and powers models like GPT, was introduced in a 2017 paper titled 'Attention Is All You Need' by researchers from which company?

Often referred to as one of the 'Godfathers of AI,' this British-Canadian cognitive psychologist and computer scientist is renowned for his foundational work on artificial neural networks and deep learning. Who is he?
Developed in the mid-1960s at MIT, which early natural language processing computer program simulated a Rogerian psychotherapist by rephrasing user input as questions?
What is the name of the AI model developed by Google DeepMind that defeated the world champion in the game of Go?
What term did John McCarthy coin in 1955 to describe "the science and engineering of making intelligent machines"?
While originally designed for rendering graphics, which hardware component became crucial for accelerating the training of deep learning models due to its parallel processing capabilities?
In 1997, IBM's Deep Blue became the first computer program to defeat a reigning world chess champion in a match. Who was that champion?
Which robotics company is famous for developing advanced humanoid robots like Atlas and dog-like robots such as Spot?
Who is widely credited with proposing the concept of the 'Turing Test' to assess a machine's ability to exhibit intelligent behavior?
Which science fiction author formulated the 'Three Laws of Robotics'?
The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined in 1956 at a workshop held at which American university?

options: A robot may not injure a human being or, through inaction, allow a human being to come to harm.
A robot must obey orders given it by human beings except where such orders would conflict with the First Law.
A robot must protect its own existence as long as such protection does not conflict with the First or Second Laws.
A robot must always identify itself as a human being when interacting with humans.
questions.question: Isaac Asimov, a prolific science fiction writer, formulated three 'Laws of Robotics' to govern the behavior of robots. Which of these is NOT one of his original three laws?
What is the primary function of a 'GPU' (Graphics Processing Unit) in modern AI and machine learning applications?
Google DeepMind's AlphaGo famously defeated the world champion of which ancient board game in 2016?
Often considered one of the "founding fathers of AI," this American computer scientist coined the term "Artificial Intelligence" in 1956 and was a co-founder of the MIT AI Lab. Who is he?

Which British mathematician proposed the "Imitation Game," later known as the Turing Test, to determine a machine's ability to exhibit intelligent behavior equivalent to, or indistinguishable from, that of a human?
Which computer program famously defeated world chess champion Garry Kasparov in 1997?

Before GPT-3 and GPT-4, OpenAI released a groundbreaking language model in 2019 that was initially deemed "too dangerous to release" due to its advanced text generation capabilities. What was it called?
What is the name of the first robot to be granted citizenship by a country?
What type of machine learning involves an agent learning to make decisions by performing actions in an environment and receiving rewards or penalties?
